business.headline: What's the one big hurdle delaying India-US trade deal? Piyush Goyal explainsPiyush Goyal said India will bring the US trade deal into force only after Washington creates a tariff framework that favours Indian exporters over regional competitors. The position follows US legal changes that overturned reciprocal tariffs and forced both sides to revisit implementation.

Sebi may allow celebrity endorsements for financial brands. But here's a catchUnder the proposal, celebrities would be allowed to endorse a financial firm's brand or company name, subject to prescribed conditions, disclaimers and prior approvals.

Stock market holiday on Muharram: Are NSE and BSE open for trading on June 26?Planning to trade on Friday, June 26? With Muharram being observed across the country, many investors are wondering whether the NSE and BSE will remain open for trading?

Don't want to pay taxes online? Here's how to use the 'Pay at Bank Counter' optionNot comfortable paying taxes online? You're not alone. The Income Tax Department still allows taxpayers to pay their dues at authorised bank branches through the 'Pay at Bank Counter' facility.

India-US trade deal very close, but here's why it isn't signed yetPiyush Goyal said India and the US are close to finalising their trade deal. India wants tariff terms that give its exporters a clear edge over rivals.

Dalal Street pares gains: Sensex ends flat after climbing over 600 pointsEarly optimism driven by lower crude oil prices and a stronger rupee faded as investors booked profits, though benchmark indices still managed to end in positive territory.

Zerodha's Nithin Kamath has a warning for you against pyramid schemesAccording to figures cited by Kamath, more than 5.5 crore Indians have lost their savings to over 5,300 such schemes. The estimated losses stood at around Rs 10 lakh crore as of 2015, and he suggested that the actual figure could be much higher now.

Gold loses over Rs 51,000 from all-time high. Why is the yellow metal crashing?Gold has fallen sharply from its peak as silver also comes under selling pressure. The correction is being driven by rate-hike bets, a stronger dollar and shifting investor appetite.

United Spirits may cut 100 jobs as Diageo looks to trim costsUnited Spirits is set to lay off around 100 employees as Diageo pursues a global cost-cutting programme. The move comes amid a wider restructuring drive in India despite strong growth and continued investment in premium brands.

Amazon to invest $48 billion in India by 2030, CEO Andy Jassy meets PM ModiAccording to Jassy, the company aims to support 3.8 million jobs in India by 2030 and help generate $80 billion in cumulative e-commerce exports.

Sensex jumps over 600 points: Why are stock markets rallying today?Benchmark indices extended their recovery on Thursday as softer crude, firm Asian cues and gains in banks and autos lifted sentiment. The move has eased near-term macro concerns, though investors remain watchful of the monsoon and global central bank signals.

EPFO portal shutdown from June 26-28: How it will affect your EPF claimsPlanning to withdraw money from your EPF account or submit a claim this week? You may have to postpone it. EPFO has announced a three-day shutdown of key portal services as it upgrades its claims processing system.

GTA 5 made $10 billion. Here's what GTA 6 could earn if it repeats historyGrand Theft Auto V is the second best-selling video game ever made. As pre-orders open for its successor, here is what the numbers say about how big GTA 6 could really be.

7 reasons why you must file ITR even if your income falls below exemption limitThink your income is too low to require an ITR? Not necessarily. Certain financial transactions can make return filing mandatory even if your income is below the exemption limit.

Explained: Why this investor calls India's stock market a poison marketAfter years of making money in the stock market, ace investor Shankar Sharma has delivered one of his strongest warnings yet. Here's why he believes Dalal Street is becoming a poison market.

Oil at pre-Iran war lows lifts Dalal Street; Sensex and Nifty jumpThe S&P BSE Sensex climbed 447.36 points, or 0.58%, to 77,438.58, while the NSE Nifty50 advanced 136.90 points, or 0.57%, to 24,158.55 as of 9:28 am.

RBI says banks must notify pensioners before recovering excess pensionThe RBI has told banks to notify government pensioners before recovering any excess pension paid into their accounts. The amendment seeks to stop sudden debits and set a clearer recovery process through notice and consent.

Lost money to online fraud? RBI's new rule might just save youThe RBI has set new rules on who bears losses from fraudulent electronic banking transactions. Customers who report quickly can get full reversals or capped compensation under a time-bound process.

Stock mismatch, mega offshore deals: What ED found during raids on Rajesh ExportsThe Enforcement Directorate searched nine premises linked to Rajesh Exports in Bengaluru and Mumbai under FEMA. The agency says missing records, stock gaps, trade set-offs and suspicious trades are now under investigation.

SK Hynix surpasses Samsung as South Korea's most valuable company amid AI chip boomSK Hynix has emerged as one of the biggest beneficiaries thanks to its leadership in high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips, a key component used in advanced AI systems.

entertainment.headline: Chiranjeevi, Nagarjuna, Trisha at Khushbu Sundar's daughter's wedding; Vijay skips Several stars, including Chiranjeevi, Nagarjuna Akkineni, Trisha Krishnan among others, attended Khushbu Sundar's daughter's wedding in Goa. Take a look at the videos here.

Ila Arun backs Alka Yagnik after viral wheelchair video: Art lives in the soul Singer Ila Arun has defended singer Alka Yagnik after comments doubted her ability to sing following a viral wheelchair video and the singer's health update. Her message underlined support for Yagnik as she celebrated her Padma Bhushan and spoke about battling sensorineural hearing loss.

SRK grooves to Jhoome Jo Pathaan, DDLJ tracks at Mangalore event. Viral videos Shah Rukh Khan performed some of his tracks at a special event in Mangalore. Several videos from the event have emerged online.

Dharman: Rajinikanth begins shooting for Ashwath Marimuthu film, new poster out The makers of Dharman unveiled a new Rajinikanth poster as shooting began on Thursday. The new update regarding the film to build anticipation among the fans.

Sense and Sensibility trailer: Daisy Edgar-Jones leads new Jane Austen adaptation The first trailer introduces Daisy Edgar-Jones as Elinor Dashwood alongside the family's changing fortunes and key suitors. It sets up the sisters' bond, romantic tensions and a looming clash between Colonel Brandon and Willoughby.

Omung Kumar explains the hidden details in Lock Upp 2's prison set Filmmaker Omung Kumar has detailed how he designed a stylised prison world for Netflix's Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa. He disclosed how he aimed to create a prison unlike anything audiences had seen before, while giving the reality show a distinct visual identity.

Are Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce getting married on July 4? Here's the truth A fresh report has linked permits, hotel bookings and venue preparations to Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's rumoured New York wedding. The reported July 3 and July 4 arrangements have intensified speculation, though the couple remain publicly silent.

OG 2 announced: Pawan Kalyan, director Sujeeth expand OG universe OG 2 has been officially announced with Pawan Kalyan producing and Sujeeth shaping the sequel's story. The film is being positioned as a larger OG Universe chapter that digs into Ojas Gambheera's past.

Welcome To The Jungle box office prediction: Can Akshay score another comedy hit? Akshay Kumar returns to the Welcome franchise with Welcome To The Jungle as its advance booking opens on a cautious note. The film's box-office prospects now rest on nostalgia, spot bookings and audience word of mouth.

Dhanush congratulates Mammootty on Padma Bhushan with a cake-cutting celebration Actor Dhanush and the Om team congratulated actor Mammootty on the film's set after his Padma Bhushan honour. Photos shared by Dhanush's production company captured the celebration, including a handshake and cake-cutting.

In the Hand of Dante review: Oscar Isaac, Gal Gadot's ambitious drama loses its gripRelease Date:Wed Jun 24, 2026 For cinephiles drawn to literary adaptations and bold swings, director Julian Schnabel's In the Hand of Dante offers striking visuals and committed performances worth sampling. Does the film do justice to the noble but uneven journey through hell, purgatory and fleeting paradise? Here is our review.

Rare photos of OTT stars before they became household names Before they became OTT sensations, Jitendra Kumar, Mithila Palkar, Sumeet Vyas, Shriya Pilgaonkar and Amol Parashar had remarkably different lives. Take a look at their rare throwback photos and inspiring journeys to fame.

Ananthan Kaadu review: Arya's revenge thriller drowns in its own sub-plotsRelease Date:Thu Jun 25, 2026 Ananthan Kaadu movie review: Director Jiyen Krishnakumar's Ananthan Kaadu, starring Arya, Murali Gopy, Indrans and Vijayaraghavan, is a story about how the system neverlets you win. The film has an interesting backdrop, but fails to achieve a cohesive story.

OTT releases this week: Top picks to watch on Prime Video, JioHotstar, SonyLIV This week's OTT slate brings Perfect Family, Lingam, The Bear Season 4 and Alliance to streaming platforms. The mix spans family conflict, crime, acclaimed international drama and strategy-led reality television.

Pathaan vs Chauhaan in Ajay Devgn's Kashmir-based film, first look out Ajay Devgn's Kashmir-based action film Chauhaan has been announced with a first-look video and a punchline aimed at Pathaans. The tribute-led reveal marks his first collaboration with Aanand L Rai and has drawn attention online.

Prahaar teaser: Rajkummar Rao as Ujjwal Nikam puts Kasab trial back in focus The teaser of Prahaar-The Ujjwal Nikam Story introduces Rajkummar Rao as public prosecutor Ujjwal Nikam and centres on the Ajmal Kasab trial after the 26/11 attacks.

What's the update on the 3 Idiots sequel? Rajkumar Hirani reveals new details Rajkumar Hirani said the 3 Idiots sequel is in an early writing stage, with Aamir Khan liking the initial idea. The film will revisit Rancho, Farhan and Raju.

Lost Indian Idol, now Deepali Sahay is viral voice of Tere Paas Main | Interview Singer Deepali Sahay spoke about landing Tere Paas Main with AR Rahman and revisiting Indian Idol. She said the song marks years of persistence and reflects her push to change views on Bhojpuri music.

Rajkumar Hirani defends Dunki, internet asks him to stop blaming the audience Director Rajkumar Hirani has defended Dunki, saying its story of illegal immigration had a narrower appeal in India. He said viewers abroad connected more deeply with the film because they understood the hardships and regret tied to migration.

general news.headline: I like Ketan, want to marry him: What Siya told parents before wedding was fixedParents of Siya Goyal, the prime accused in the murder of her fiance, said she repeatedly told them she liked Ketan Agarwal and wanted to marry him.

Deniz Undav's outrageous World Cup numbers keep growing, yet Julian Nagelsmann still refuses to hand him a start. Ivory Coast vs Curacao line-ups are out too.

Iran launched projectile that hit cargo ship trying to pass Hormuz: ReportThe attack is seen as the first major test of the US-Iran agreement signed last week to end hostilities and reopen the strategically vital shipping lane.

Why Nihang Sikhs have gathered at Uttarakhand border and what's behind the rowHundreds of Nihang Sikhs gathered at the Uttarakhand-Himachal Pradesh border after talks with the state administration failed over the Karnaprayag clash and Nagrasu Gurdwara standoff, prompting heavy security deployment and heightened vigil.

In a first, Canada says Khalistanis planted Air India bomb. Why this is big for IndiaFor the first time, Canada's intelligence agency has explicitly blamed Khalistani extremists for the 1985 Air India bombing — an attack New Delhi has long described as a Khalistani terror plot. The acknowledgement marks a major shift in Ottawa's stance and could reshape how Canada addresses Khalistani extremism and its ties with India.

8 arrested hours after FIR filed in alleged Ram temple donation theftThe Uttar Pradesh Police on Thursday arrested all eight accused in the alleged Ram Temple donation embezzlement case, hours after registering the first FIR based on the preliminary findings of a Special Investigation Team (SIT).

Govt hikes passport fees from July 1, new one to cost Rs 2,500, Tatkaal Rs 5,000From July 1, a new 36-page passport or its reissue for applicants aged 18 years and above will cost Rs 2,500 under the normal scheme, up from Rs 1,500 at present. The Tatkaal fee has been raised to Rs 5,000 from Rs 3,500.

Apple hikes MacBook, iPad prices by 1 lakh in India: Check new prices hereApple has raised MacBook and iPad prices in India by 1 lakh. The move puts focus on the updated price list for buyers tracking the latest costs. Here is the full list of new prices that are already live on the company's Apple India website.

UP man falls off balcony while leaning to grab ice cream from street vendorA man in Uttar Pradesh's Sonbhadra fell from his first-floor balcony while trying to take an ice cream from a street vendor below.

Lohagad trip was Ketan's idea: Siya's mother claims she did not want to go trekkingSiya Goyal's parents said Ketan Agarwal insisted on the Lohagad Fort trek despite her reluctance. They claimed phone chats support their account and show the outing was not her plan.

Live: Turkey, Venezuela discuss aid as toll in twin earthquakes climbs to 188Venezuela Earthquake News: Two strong earthquakes struck Venezuela within less than a minute early on Thursday, causing extensive damage and prompting evacuations in Caracas. The first quake, measured at magnitude 7.1 by the US Geological Survey, originated near the coastal town of Moron, about 168 kilometres west of the capital, at a shallow depth of 13 kilometres. Around 40 seconds later, a stronger 7.5-magnitude tremor hit the same area at a depth of 10 kilometres, worsening the destruction. Buildings suffered structural damage, walls collapsed, and clouds of dust spread across affected neighbourhoods as emergency teams began rescue and assessment operations. Authorities warned that injuries were likely and cautioned residents about possible aftershocks. Officials described the event as the strongest seismic activity to affect the country in roughly a century. Tsunami alerts issued for Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands were later withdrawn. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Iran wants Gulf countries on board to collect $40 billion in Hormuz transit feesIran is now pursuing an ambitious plan that it believes could generate as much as $40 billion a year by charging ships for security, safety and environmental services in the strategic waterway.

A Harrapan passport? The curious history of a 4,000-year-old 'passport seal'If Harappan terracotta seals did function as travel markers, they may represent one of the earliest clues to the passport's long history in the subcontinent. From the Indus world to the Mauryans, the Mughals and the British, the document changed shape – seal, sanad, paper – but its purpose stayed much the same: to identify, permit and watch.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to get India debut in Belfast T20I? Batting coach breaks silenceIndia batting coach Sitanshu Kotak said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will get his chance when the time is right, insisting the team will not drop an in-form player just to hand the 15-year-old his debut.

Death toll climbs to 188 after Venezuela earthquakes; hundreds still trappedSurvivors described scenes "like a horror movie" after twin earthquakes -- the most powerful to strike Venezuela in over a century -- devastated parts of the Latin American country. Buildings collapsed, families searched through rubble for loved ones, and rescuers worked through the night amid fears of high casualties.

Monsoon to lash India on Friday as Assam, Bihar brace for fierce stormsThe IMD has forecast heavy rain, thunderstorms and squally seas as the southwest monsoon strengthens across India on Friday. The pattern keeps flood and landslide risks high in the Northeast even as east Uttar Pradesh remains under a heat-wave warning.

Why Khamenei funeral invite for PM Modi puts India in a delicate spotIran has invited Prime Minister Narendra Modi to attend Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's state funeral. How New Delhi responds to the invitation could reveal how it plans to balance ties with Iran, the US and Israel. It's a tightrope walk for New Delhi for sure.

Untidy India scrape past Bangladesh to keep semifinal hopes aliveWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs BAN: India beat Bangladesh by five wickets in Manchester on Thursday, but will need to improve significantly in several areas of their game before facing Australia in a crucial Group A clash at Lord's on Sunday.

Senior IPS officer Mahesh Dixit named new Intelligence Bureau chiefSenior IPS officer Mahesh Dixit has been appointed as the new Director of the Intelligence Bureau, bringing decades of experience in intelligence operations, counter-terrorism and internal security.

Siya is mini Sonam: Raja Raghuvanshi's mother compares Pune case to son's murderRaja Raghuvanshi's mother has compared Pune fort murder accused Siya Goyal to her daughter-in-law Sonam Raghuvanshi, the prime accused in the 2025 Meghalaya honeymoon murder case.

Alka Yagnik reacts to viral wheelchair video, assures fans amid health concernAlka Yagnik addressed concern after a wheelchair video from the Padma ceremony went viral. She said she was tired after the event and is recovering well.

Why police verification for passport if it proves nothing? 5 big questionsAn MEA official reiterated that a passport is a "travel document" and not a "citizenship document". The clarification settles one debate about the passport, but opens up five larger questions about verification, ownership, surrender, and citizenship itself.

After UAE, Iraq plans to quit Saudi-dictated OPEC. How big will be the jolt?After the UAE's exit, Iraq has become the latest OPEC member to challenge the oil cartel's production quotas. Iraq, the second-largest crude producer in the OPEC, has hinted at an exit unless its output ceiling is raised. The dispute threatens to deepen cracks within the Saudi Arabia-controlled alliance.

At what price does E85 start making sense? The economics behind ethanol fuelE85 fuel is cheaper at the pump, but its lower energy density increases consumption. So, at what price does it actually become cheaper to run than E20? We crunch the numbers.

sports.headline: Mohammad Amir starts afresh as British citizen, joins T20 Blast as domestic player Mohammad Amir has begun a new phase of his career after joining Notts Outlaws in the T20 Blast as a domestic player following the acquisition of British citizenship, allowing him to play county cricket without occupying an overseas slot.

South Africa will be looking to put up a strong show against the Dutch and strengthen their chances of qualifying for the semis.

Untidy India scrape past Bangladesh to keep semifinal hopes alive Women's T20 World Cup, IND vs BAN: India beat Bangladesh by five wickets in Manchester on Thursday, but will need to improve significantly in several areas of their game before facing Australia in a crucial Group A clash at Lord's on Sunday.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's first Indian training session overshadowed by poor facilities IRE vs IND: India's first training session in Belfast was briefly disrupted by poor practice facilities before officials stepped in. The noise soon moved to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, whose calm first senior nets drew the diaspora and underlined the scrutiny around him.

Dead man rising? Ghanaian witch doctor lifts curse over England's Harry Kane FIFA World Cup 2026: Nana Kwaku Bonsam says he has lifted the supposed curse placed on Harry Kane after England's draw with Ghana. The claim keeps an absurd subplot alive, but England's real issue remains finishing chances on the pitch.

Keeping his case for the number 3 spot alive? Sai Sudharsan smashes 100 against SL A Sai Sudharsan hit 132 for India A against Sri Lanka A in the ongoing unofficial Test series. The innings revived his Test No. 3 case and justified management's patience amid criticism.

Who is Jai Moondra? Born in Rajasthan, Ireland pacer eager to debut against India IRE vs IND: Left-arm seamer Jai Moondra, who was born in Rajasthan in 1997, is in line to make his Ireland debut against India when the two sides meet in a two-match T20I series starting on June 26.

IND vs BAN Highlights: India remains alive, beat Bangladesh by 5 wickets India (IND-W) vs Bangladesh (BAN-W) Highlights, Women's T20 World Cup: India remain alive in the race to qualify for the semifinals as they beat Bangladesh by five wickets in Manchester with 19 balls to spare.

Remember who you are! Neymar draws inspiration from Lewis Hamilton after Brazil returnFIFA World Cup 2026: Neymar returned for Brazil against Scotland after 981 days and echoed Lewis Hamilton's mantra. The comeback answered fresh mockery and reopened the question – whether his career still has one more act, one last dance left.

Trouble for India? Australia may get huge boost for high-voltage Lord's clashWomen's T20 World Cup: Australia head coach Shelley Nitschke said that Phoebe Litchfield may return against India on June 28 at the Lord's, saying that that left-handed batter has recovered well from her calf injury.

Asian Games silver medallist Yarraji targets gold after comeback from ACL injuryJyothi Yarraji marked her return from an ACL tear with a 12.99-second hurdles win at the National Inter-State Championships. A year after injury and surgery, India's record holder now believes Asian Games gold is within reach.

Why Jannik Sinner wore a cooling vest at training ahead of WimbledonJannik Sinner used a cooling vest during Wimbledon preparations due to the London heatwave, which has also impacted tournament operations, causing a Hawk-Eye outage and drawing criticism from players including Eva Lys.

Monumental mess? Lionel Messi's 85-foot statue in Argentina divides fansArgentina has unveiled an 85-foot Lionel Messi statue in Cutral Co. Its trophy placement and unfinished lower half have triggered ridicule online, even as locals celebrate the monument's tourist pull.

Tennis players want more money, and they are scapegoating media for itThe prize money controversy from the French Open is set to boil over to another Grand Slam. In a show of defiance, top tennis players in the world are set to limit their press interactions to 15 minutes at Wimbledon.

Manika Batra's Asian Games doors remain shut: Table tennis federation sourceThe Table Tennis Federation of India have shut the doors on Manika Batra's selection to the main squad for the Asian Games. TTFI sources claim that due process was followed during the selection process.

Food poisoning or what? Korean media tear into coach after shock World Cup lossSouth Korea manager Hong Myung-bo came under heavy criticism from the country's media after his side's lifeless 0-1 defeat to South Africa. The loss left them staring at an early FIFA World Cup exit.

Neymar's son mistaken for pitch invader, Brazil star steps in to clear confusionFIFA World Cup 2026: Before sharing a heartwarming hug with his father, Neymar's son, Davi Lucca, was stopped from entering the field after Brazil's 3-0 win over Scotland in Miami. Stewards mistook him for a pitch invader, but the Brazil star quickly stepped in to clear up the confusion.

Watch: India 4x100 women beat China in China for gold in Asian relay meetIndia's women's 4x100m relay team delivered a tactical masterclass to stun hosts China and clinch Gold at the Asian Relay Championships. It was a big boost to the quartet of SS Sneha, Sudeshna Shivankar, Tamanna, and veteran anchor Srabani Nanda ahead of a busy season.

World Cup legend Guillermo Ochoa gets storybook ending, retires at the AztecaGuillermo Ochoa got the farewell he had dreamed of at the Azteca Stadium as Mexico completed a perfect FIFA World Cup group-stage campaign with a 3-0 win over Czechia. Making what is expected to be his final World Cup appearance, the cult hero soaked in the applause of more than 80,000 fans who stood to celebrate one of the tournament's most recognisable figures.

Meet Srabani Nanda: The 35-year-old sprinter who refuses to slow downAt 35, veteran Odisha sprinter Srabani Nanda is redefining longevity in Indian athletics, anchoring the national relay team to fresh continental success in China while championing a fierce, lifelong commitment to clean sport.

From accidental goalkeeper to India's best: Bichu Devi dreams of LA28 gloryFrom taking up hockey and goalkeeping via accident, Bichu Devi Kharibam established herself as India's best shotstopper in hockey at the moment. With LA Olympics dream in mind, the 25-year-old aims to continue growing under the tutelage of Savita Punia.

FIFA World Cup: South Africa achieve historic knockout qualificationSouth Africa scripted history at the FIFA World Cup after beating South Korea in their final group-stage match to qualify for the knockout rounds for the first time. Thapelo Maseko scored the decisive goal in the second half as Hugo Broos' side overcame a difficult start to the tournament.

Wyatt-Hodge helps England reach semis with West Indies winDanni Wyatt-Hodge drove England to a 38-run win over West Indies at Lord's and into the semi-finals. The result avenged England's 2024 group-stage loss and left West Indies needing to beat Ireland to qualify.

981 days in injury exile, Neymar brought to tears on Brazil returnBrazil samba-ed their way into the knockouts of the FIFA World Cup with a statement 3-0 win against Scotland. Neymar Jr. walked into the pitch for the national team after an injury-exile of 981 days.

T20 World Cup: Bruised India face tricky Bangladesh test in must-win gameWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs BAN: India meet Bangladesh in a must-win Women's T20 World Cup match at Old Trafford after defeat to South Africa. The result could turn on India's fragile middle order, Bangladesh's spinners and the pressure of qualification.

Morocco overcomes Haiti scare to enter WC 2026 knockouts as Group C runners-upMorocco beat Haiti 4-2 in an instant World Cup classic to finish second in Group C. In a match that saw four goals in the first half, the Atlas Lions were able to battle on score two in the final 45 minutes to get the victory.

Neymar's return, Vini Jr's night: Brazil head to knockouts with 3-0 win vs ScotlandSCO vs BRA, FIFA World Cup 2026: Vinicius Jr scored twice as Brazil beat Scotland 3-0 to top Group C, while Neymar made his long-awaited return from injury in front of a packed Miami Stadium to cap a memorable night.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
